description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone base and a wooden roof supported by two vertical wooden posts. The roof is gabled and constructed from weathered wooden planks, providing shelter over the well opening. A wooden crank mechanism is mounted horizontally between the posts, with a rope wound around it and a wooden bucket attached for drawing water. The stonework of the well is irregular, giving it a rustic, hand-built appearance, and the wood shows signs of aging and exposure to the elements. The well's interior is open, allowing access to the water below, and the bucket hangs centrally within the opening. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements, focusing on function and durability.
description: A rustic stone water well with a gabled wooden roof, crank mechanism, and hanging bucket. The structure is weathered and functional, with no decorative features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the gabled wooden roof, stone base, and partial view of the crank mechanism behind the support post.
description_view_1: Side view clearly displaying the wooden crank, rope, and bucket hanging inside the well, with the full width of the roof visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the gabled roof and stone base, but the crank and bucket are less visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view to view 1, again showing the crank, rope, and bucket, with the stone base and roof structure prominent.
attribute_well_cover_design: Gabled wooden roof made of planks, no lid or slab directly covering the well opening.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: Visible wooden crank with rope and wooden bucket suspended in the well opening.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately waist-high, forming the main protective barrier around the well.
